commonMistakes: Memorizing syntax without predicting the underlying value or execution order.
Mutating shared data when the task expects a new value or explicit ownership.
Treating browser APIs and language features as the same layer.
Choosing an abstraction from habit rather than required operations.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Explain functions calling functions using a new example and justify the result.
Explain function inputs and outputs using a new example and justify the result.
Apply function review and composition using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Functions calling functions** → **Function inputs and outputs** → **Function review and composition**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: Functions calling functions is best understood as follows: Functions can delegate to other functions, turning a large operation into named, testable transformations. The caller coordinates the sequence while each helper owns one responsibility.

choices.feedback: Function inputs and outputs is best understood as follows: Parameters define what a function needs; return values define what it produces. Explicit inputs and outputs reduce hidden dependencies and make functions easier to compose and test.

choices.label: HTML describes structure, CSS describes presentation, and JavaScript expresses behavior and application logic. It responds to events, changes the document, communicates with servers, and coordinates state.

choices.feedback: Function review and composition is best understood as follows: Composition builds larger behavior from smaller functions whose outputs can become later inputs. Even without a composition utility, the principle encourages narrow responsibilities and predictable data flow.
choices.label: Composition builds larger behavior from smaller functions whose outputs can become later inputs. Even without a composition utility, the principle encourages narrow responsibilities and predictable data flow.

choices.feedback: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.

summary: Functions Working Together connects Functions calling functions, Function inputs and outputs, Function review and composition.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Functions Working Together

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: The ideas in functions working together recur throughout later JavaScript work, reducing memorization and debugging later. It belongs to the **Orientation and Foundations** module.
